pattern: Map each integer 1..n to its digit sum, tally group sizes in a hash map, then count how many groups share the maximum tally.
hints: Digit sum of a number is cheap to compute; you can do it with repeated mod-10 and integer division.
Accumulate a frequency map from digit-sum to count of integers that have that digit sum.
Once the map is built, one linear scan finds the maximum group size, and another (or the same) scan counts how many groups reached that maximum.

edge_inputs.description: n=1: single element, single group of size 1
n=2: {1}->1, {2}->1 — two groups tied at size 1
n=9: digits 1..9 all distinct, 9 groups each of size 1
n=10: digit-sum groups sizes start to differ
n=100: 100 appears with digit sum 1, same group as 1, 10
n=999: many numbers collapse to same digit sums

stress.description: random n in [1, 10000] — covers full input range
